steps.title: Identify the example
steps.content: The first sentence says potters consider two factors when choosing clay: appearance and intended use. The next sentence gives a specific case: earthenware is used for decorative pieces because of its appearance, but not often in industrial settings because of durability.

steps.title: Eliminate illogical transitions
steps.content: Choice A, “In other words,” would restate the previous idea, but the sentence gives a case. Choice B, “Regardless,” signals contrast or dismissal that is not present. Choice C, “In conclusion,” would wrap up an argument too early. The transition should introduce a specific case of the two factors. The answer is D.
